views.definition:
SELECT duty_log_id,
    crew_id,
    flight_id,
    duty_start,
    duty_end
   FROM dutylog;

entity_relationships.related_entities: CrewRank, CrewBase, License, AircraftType, Flight, Roster, DutyLog, LeaveRequest, ExpenseClaim, Training, ComplianceViolation, Notification
entity_relationships.business_meaning: A Crew member belongs to one CrewRank and one CrewBase; holds multiple Licenses (many-to-many), is rated on multiple AircraftTypes (many-to-many), is assigned to many Flights via Roster, accumulates DutyLogs, files LeaveRequests and ExpenseClaims, enrolls in Training programs, may incur ComplianceViolations, and receives Notifications (all one-to-many from Crew)
A CrewRank (e.g., Captain, First Officer) classifies many Crew members (one-to-many)
A CrewBase (home airport/hub) is assigned to many Crew members (one-to-many)
Licenses are held by Crew members through a many-to-many relationship; each crew member can have multiple licenses and each license type can be held by many crew members

AircraftTypes are the rated qualifications of Crew (many-to-many) and the aircraft flown on Flights; also referenced by Simulator sessions and Training programs (one-to-many to Flights and TrainingSessions)
A Flight uses one AircraftType and is associated with Crew members via Roster assignments and DutyLog entries (one-to-many to Roster and DutyLog)
A Roster is a schedule plan that links Crew members to Flights through CrewAssignment (one Roster to many Crew-to-Flight assignments)
A DutyLog records actual duty time for a Crew member, optionally linked to a specific Flight (one-to-many from Crew and Flight)
A LeaveRequest is filed by a Crew member and categorized by one LeaveType (one-to-many from Crew, many LeaveTypes serve many requests)
An ExpenseClaim is submitted by a Crew member and categorized by one ExpenseType (one-to-many from Crew, many ExpenseTypes serve many claims)
A TrainingProgram contains many TrainingSessions; Crew members enroll in sessions; SimulatorSessions link Crew to specific AircraftTypes (hierarchical one-to-many with many-to-many enrollments)
A ComplianceViolation is recorded against a single Crew member, tracking regulatory or policy breaches (one-to-many from Crew)
Notifications are messages dispatched to individual Crew members (one-to-many from Crew)

business_processes.tables: medicalcertificate, trainingenrollment, simulatorsession, crewaircraftrating, crewlicense, trainingsession, trainingprogram
license, aircrafttype, leavetype, expensetype, crewbase, crewrank

use_cases.name: Crew Rostering and Flight Assignment
use_cases.description: Plan and assign crew members to flights based on their rank, base, aircraft ratings, license validity, and rest period compliance to build optimized rosters.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewassignment, roster, flight, crewaircraftrating, crewlicense, restperiod

use_cases.name: Training and Certification Management
use_cases.description: Schedule training programs, enroll crew into sessions, track completion, and ensure simulator and certification requirements are met for aircraft type ratings.
use_cases.involved_tables: trainingprogram, trainingsession, trainingenrollment, simulatorsession, crew, aircrafttype

use_cases.name: Compliance and Duty Time Monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor crew duty hours, rest periods, medical certificate validity, and log compliance violations to ensure regulatory adherence.

use_cases.name: Leave and Expense Administration
use_cases.description: Process crew leave requests by type and review/approve expense claims categorized by expense type, including notification of status changes.
use_cases.involved_tables: leaverequest, leavetype, expenseclaim, expensetype, notification, crew

use_cases.name: Crew Profile and Qualification Lookup
use_cases.description: Retrieve comprehensive crew profiles including rank, base, licenses, aircraft ratings, contact information, and emergency contacts for operational reference.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewrank, crewbase, crewlicense, license, crewaircraftrating, crewcontact, crewemergencycontact

sample_queries.question: List all crew members assigned to a specific flight along with their rank and base.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cr.rank_name, cb.base_name, f.flight_number, f.departure_time
FROM crew c
JOIN crewassignment ca ON c.crew_id = ca.crew_id
JOIN flight f ON ca.flight_id = f.flight_id
JOIN crewrank cr ON c.rank_id = cr.rank_id
JOIN crewbase cb ON c.base_id = cb.base_id
WHERE f.flight_id = :flight_id
ORDER BY cr.rank_name, c.last_name;

sample_queries.question: Find all crew members whose licenses expire within the next 90 days.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, l.license_name, cl.expiry_date, cl.expiry_date - CURRENT_DATE AS days_until_expiry
FROM crew c
JOIN crewlicense cl ON c.crew_id = cl.crew_id
JOIN license l ON cl.license_id = l.license_id
WHERE cl.expiry_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE AND CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '90 days'
ORDER BY cl.expiry_date;

sample_queries.question: Calculate total flight hours logged by each crew member in the current month.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(dl.duration_hours) AS total_duty_hours
FROM crew c
JOIN dutylog dl ON c.crew_id = dl.crew_id
WHERE dl.log_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND dl.log_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
GROUP BY c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_duty_hours DESC;

sample_queries.question: Identify crew members with active compliance violations and their assigned base.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cb.base_name, cv.violation_type, cv.violation_date, cv.severity, cv.status
FROM crew c
JOIN complianceviolation cv ON c.crew_id = cv.crew_id
JOIN crewbase cb ON c.base_id = cb.base_id
WHERE cv.status = 'Open'
ORDER BY cv.severity DESC, cv.violation_date DESC;

sample_queries.question: Show training completion status for all crew enrolled in a specific training program.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, tp.program_name, ts.session_date, te.completion_status, te.score
FROM trainingenrollment te
JOIN crew c ON te.crew_id = c.crew_id
JOIN trainingsession ts ON te.session_id = ts.session_id
JOIN trainingprogram tp ON ts.program_id = tp.program_id
WHERE tp.program_id = :program_id
ORDER BY ts.session_date DESC, c.last_name;

schema_patterns.description: Columns tracking creation and modification timestamps and users
